title: Initial TC Skill Implementation

description.summary: Complete implementation of the TC (Technical Change) skill for structured code change tracking with JSON records, accessible HTML output, and AI session handoff
description.motivation: User manages 10+ projects and needs a universal, bulletproof change tracking system so that when an AI session expires or is abandoned, the next bot can seamlessly resume work. Also serves as human-readable documentation of all code changes.
description.detailed_design: 7-phase build: JSON schemas first, then Python validator with state machine, accessible CSS/HTML templates, Python generators (stdlib only), init templates for project onboarding, SKILL.md with 8 commands, and example data. Each generated HTML file is self-contained with inlined CSS for file:// compatibility.

files_affected.description: Complete JSON Schema for TC records — all fields, types, constraints, enums
JSON Schema for the project-level TC registry (master index)
JSON Schema for project-specific TC configuration
Python validator with state machine enforcement, schema validation, TC ID generation, and CLI
Accessible dark theme CSS — WCAG AA+, rem-based, high contrast, print stylesheet
HTML template skeleton for individual TC record pages
HTML template skeleton for dashboard with CSS-only filters
Python generator: reads tc_record.json, validates, inlines CSS, outputs accessible HTML
Python generator: reads registry + records, builds dashboard with metrics and CSS-only filters
CLAUDE.md template with mandatory session start protocol, auto-tracking rules, and commands
Complete skill definition with 8 commands, auto-detection rules, validation rules, and state machine docs
Realistic example TC with all fields populated — authentication feature for Cards_bot
Example registry with 3 TCs in different states
Git ignore for Python cache, IDE files, generated HTML
Preview server config for local HTML testing

test_cases.title: Example TC record passes schema validation
procedure: Check exit code is 0 and output is VALID
test_cases.expected_result: Validator outputs VALID with exit code 0

test_cases.title: Example registry passes schema validation
test_cases.expected_result: Validator outputs VALID with exit code 0

test_cases.title: TC HTML generator produces valid output
procedure: Verify CSS is inlined (dark theme variables present)
test_cases.expected_result: HTML file generated with inlined CSS, dark theme, and all sections populated
test_cases.actual_result: Generated examples/example_tc_record.html (1188 lines), CSS fully inlined with --bg: #0d0d18, all sections rendered

evidence.content:
python generators/generate_tc_html.py examples/example_tc_record.json --output examples/example_tc_record.html
Generated: examples\example_tc_record.html

test_cases.title: Dashboard generator produces valid output
procedure: Verify HTML file is created with status metrics and TC cards
test_cases.expected_result: Dashboard HTML with status stats, distribution bar, filter bar, and TC cards
test_cases.actual_result: Generated examples/example_dashboard.html (973 lines), all sections present

evidence.content:
python generators/generate_dashboard.py examples/example_tc_registry.json --output examples/example_dashboard.html
Generated: examples\example_dashboard.html

handoff.progress_summary: Full TC skill implementation complete. All 7 phases done. 16 files created. Validator, generators, and examples all verified. Published to GitHub at Elkidogz/technical-change-skill. Dogfooding by creating this TC record for the skill itself.
next_steps: Deploy TC tracking to other projects in AI BOTS directory using /tc init
User to approve and close this TC (transition to deployed)
Consider adding README.md to the GitHub repo for public documentation
key_context: Python 3.13.6 on Windows 11, all generators use stdlib only
User has low vision — all HTML is WCAG AA+ with rem-based fonts
Dark theme matches Cards_bot design: #0d0d18 bg, #d4af37 gold

decisions_made.decision: Python stdlib only — no external dependencies
decisions_made.rationale: Ensures portability across all 10+ projects without dependency management overhead
Self-contained HTML files that work from file:// URLs without a server

decisions_made.decision: Append-only revision history rather than archived snapshots
decisions_made.rationale: Simpler to manage, single file per TC, no file proliferation

decisions_made.decision: Session handoff embedded in TC record rather than separate file
decisions_made.rationale: Everything about a TC in one place — the next bot reads one file to get full context

notes: This is the first TC ever created by the TC skill — tracking its own implementation. A meta-record that serves as both documentation and validation of the system.
